mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue expectations, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as equity investors price in higher earnings.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when PAYEMS increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding 3% year-over-year erodes real corporate margins and prompts forward-looking investors to demand higher risk premia, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds rate is held below 0.25% while unemployment exceeds 5.5%, the central bank's commitment to maximum employment overrides inflation concerns, pushing investors into equities and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of joint occurrences of FEDFUNDS below 0.25% and UNRATE above 5.5%.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery, quarterly GDP growth from a depressed base signals rebounding corporate revenues and consumer demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters during post-recession recovery when GDPC1 increases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production signals manufacturing sector stress, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as traders downgrade near-term earnings.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month by more than 0.5%
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO declines more than 0.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand acceleration, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when both RSAFS and HOUST increase month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating household income and collapsing consumer spending, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as recession probabilities are repriced higher.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points in a single month
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances when UNRATE jumps more than 0.2pp month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales demonstrates that wage income is translating directly into consumption expenditure, creating a self-reinforcing demand loop that produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when both PAYEMS and RSAFS increase month-over-month.
